criteria_results.description: home.tsx contains no static import statements for Canvas, Toolbar, ObjectToolbar, LayerPanel, or PropertiesPanel — each is declared via React.lazy(() => import(...))
criteria_results.evidence: Verified in client/src/pages/home.tsx lines 5-9: all five components are declared using React.lazy with named export mapping.

criteria_results.description: home.tsx wraps the editor-branch JSX (rendered when pdfFile is not null) in <React.Suspense> with a non-null fallback prop
criteria_results.evidence:
Verified in client/src/pages/home.tsx lines 20-41: The main editor UI is wrapped in
...
}>.

criteria_results.description: PDFUploader remains a static import in home.tsx (not lazy)
criteria_results.evidence: Verified in client/src/pages/home.tsx line 3: import {PDFUploader} from '@/components/editor/PDFUploader';

criteria_results.description: Canvas.tsx sets pdfjs.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc using a local ?url import, not a string referencing unpkg.com or any other CDN
criteria_results.evidence: Verified in client/src/components/editor/Canvas.tsx lines 18-21: import pdfjsWorkerSrc from 'pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs?url'; and pdfjs.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = pdfjsWorkerSrc;

criteria_results.description: Running `npm run build` produces a separate JS chunk containing pdfjs-dist that is NOT part of the main entry chunk
criteria_results.evidence: Ran npm run build. Output shows separate chunks: pdf.worker.min-*.mjs (1,046 kB) and Canvas-*.js (481 kB). The main index-*.js chunk is 441 kB.

suggestions: The spinner in Suspense fallback is centered on the whole screen. It might be better to have a fallback that matches the editor layout to prevent layout shift, but for performance improvement this is sufficient.
